subject: entity.product.tap
claim: Tap underperformance in Brazil combines weak attach rate, SMB upsell/product adoption issues, Tap + Loyalty reconciliation trust problems, and delayed backend fixes tied to schema migration work.

subject: customer.acai_express
claim: Açaí Express escalation is primarily a Tap + Loyalty / Loyalty reconciliation issue rooted in legacy Brazil merchant references; Confluence matters because merchant_id migration controls durable fix timing.

subject: customer.cosmos_hotels
claim: Cosmos Hotels was lost because multi-property reporting was missing; pricing was not the issue. Confluence priorities prevented a firm product commitment, so the workaround did not satisfy the customer.
chain_steps: pricing was explicitly not the issue
Cosmos needed consolidated multi-property reporting across 47 properties
the proposed export workaround was not acceptable to the customer
the product team could not commit to the reporting fix because Confluence priorities constrained roadmap capacity
Cosmos chose a competitor at a similar price point

subject: team.brazil_customer_success
claim: Brazil CS capacity problems stem from CSM attrition/backfill gaps, higher case volume, SMB support expectations, and Tap/Loyalty reconciliation pressure. Marco Bianchi owns CS overall; Maria Santos owns Brazil CS.
